name: Gourmet Glossary 4
recipeInstructions:
1. Sucanat is a brand name sweetener made from organic sugar cane juice that has been dehydrated into a powder, retaining all its vitamins and minerals.
2. Summer savory is an annual herb with narrow green leaves and an intense aroma.
3. Tahini is a smooth, nutty-flavored paste made from raw, ground sesame seeds.
4. Tamales are cornmeal dough stuffed with Southwestern fillings, such as seasoned black beans, wrapped in corn husks, and steamed.
5. Tamari is a dark fermented soy sauce made without wheat; it is the by-product of making miso.
6. Tamarind paste is a dark paste made from the fruit of the tamarind tree; it tastes similar to a combination of dates and lemons with a bit of saltiness, often used in Indian cuisine and available in international sections of most supermarkets.
7. Tarragon is a green, licorice-flavored herb with a sweet, yet pungent aroma; excellent for flavoring vinegars and frequently used in French cooking.
8. Tofu is a smooth, pressed, white soybean curd with a mild flavor and soft consistency.
9. Turbinado sugar (Sugar in the Raw) is coarse, light-brown granulated sugar made from cane juice that has been steamed and refined to remove impurities.
10. Turmeric is a bright, golden-colored powdered spice used in many Indian and Middle Eastern dishes.
11. Vegetarian mincemeat is a sweet mixture of chopped apples, oranges, cinnamon, and nuts usually baked in a pie crust; made without the traditional addition of suet.
12. Vermouth is an aromatic wine flavored with various herbs and spices to achieve either a dry or sweet taste.
13. Walnut oil is a topaz-colored oil with a rich, nutty flavor used in salad dressings and baked goods.
14. Watercress is a delicate, mildly flavored salad green with long stems and small round or oval-shaped leaves.
